rationale: The cited paper is discussed in the related-literature section as one of the key empirical studies that established the multitier private-label phenomenon and documented cannibalization patterns across tiers. The citing paper explicitly positions its own model as "in keeping with this work," using those findings to motivate its theoretical setup (economy, midtier, and premium store brands) and contribution. This is more than a passing mention because the cited study helps frame the research problem and the specific portfolio issues the theory addresses.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the empirical line represented by Geyskens et al. (2010) by developing a theoretical vertical-differentiation/channel model for multitier store brands. It uses the cited paper's empirical evidence on tier introductions and cannibalization as motivation, then derives broader equilibrium implications for quality ordering, profits, and coordination.
